role_description: Instrument Technician working on industrial construction or refinery projects. Responsible for installing, calibrating, loop checking, and troubleshooting instrumentation systems including transmitters, control valves, analyzers, thermocouples, RTDs, level instruments, flow meters, and safety instrumented systems. Follows hook-up drawings and loop diagrams for proper installation.
report_focus: When structuring this person's reports, look for: instruments installed today, calibrations performed, loop checks completed, material or equipment issues, safety observations, quality concerns, troubleshooting activities, issues that need supervisor attention, and planned work for tomorrow.

vocabulary.description: Full journeyman-level instrumentation terms covering installation, calibration, loop testing, and all instrument types.

language_notes: Workers may mix English and Spanish. Technical instrumentation terms are usually said in English. Common terms: transmitter, valve, loop, calibration, tubing, JB (junction box).
safety_rules: All personnel must wear required PPE at all times: hard hat, safety glasses, steel-toe boots, FR clothing
Never work on energized or pressurized systems without proper isolation and LOTO procedures
Verify zero energy state before working on any instrument loop
Use only approved intrinsically safe equipment in classified areas
Report all incidents, near-misses, and unsafe conditions immediately
Attend daily toolbox talks and JSA reviews before starting work
Fall protection is required at heights of 6 feet or more
Keep work areas clean and organized
Use proper PPE when handling calibration gases
Never bypass safety instrumented systems without authorization
Pressure test all tubing connections before applying process pressure
Use proper tube bending tools — never use pliers or adjustable wrenches on compression fittings
Verify intrinsically safe barriers are properly installed before working in classified areas
Never exceed rated pressure on test equipment or instruments
Bleed all pressure from impulse lines before disconnecting
Use explosion-proof tools in Class I Division 1 areas
Verify proper grounding of instrument cable shields
Calibration gas cylinders must be secured upright and stored properly
Ground Fault Circuit Interrupters (GFCI) required for all temporary power
Confined space entry requires a permit, atmospheric testing, and a dedicated attendant
Hot work requires a permit and a fire watch for minimum 30 minutes after work completion
Report any damaged tools or equipment immediately — do not use defective equipment
Be aware of overhead hazards and never stand under suspended loads
Know your emergency evacuation routes and muster point
Never operate equipment you are not trained and authorized to use
tools_and_equipment: Fluke multimeter — for voltage, current, resistance, and continuity checks
Fluke process calibrator — mA source/measure for 4-20mA loop testing
Beamex MC6 calibrator — documenting calibrator for pressure, temperature, and electrical
HART communicator — for smart instrument configuration, diagnostics, and trim
Deadweight tester — primary pressure standard for calibration verification
Pressure hand pump — for generating pneumatic or hydraulic test pressure
Pressure modules — for different ranges, must match instrument range
RTD/TC simulator — for simulating temperature inputs during loop checks
Megger / insulation resistance tester — for cable insulation testing
Tube cutter — for clean, square cuts on instrument tubing
Tube bender — manual or hydraulic, use correct die for tubing OD
Deburring tool — for removing burrs from cut tubing
Swagelok gap gauge — for verifying proper compression fitting makeup
Torque wrench — calibrated, for proper bolt torque on flanged connections
Open-end wrenches — various sizes for fittings and process connections
Wire strippers and crimpers — for proper cable termination
Screwdrivers — flathead, Phillips, and precision for terminal screws
Allen wrench sets — metric and standard for instrument adjustments
Laptop computer — for DCS/PLC configuration software and documentation
Label maker — for proper cable and instrument identification
Cable toner and tracer — for identifying cables in trays and conduit
Level and plumb bob — for proper instrument mounting alignment
Tape measure — 25-foot minimum for field measurements
Inspection mirror — for checking connections in tight spaces
Headlamp and flashlight — for work in dark areas and cabinets
